title: helianthemum apenninum subsp. croceum
intro: Helianthemum apenninum subsp. croceum, also known as the golden rockrose, is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Cistaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions such as wounds, skin issues, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Helianthemum apenninum subsp. croceum include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting wound healing, while its herbal preparations include teas, infusions, and extracts that can be used topically or orally. By harnessing the medicinal properties of this plant, individuals can potentially experience relief from certain health issues and promote overall well-being.
